page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 112 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 56 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled at 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 448 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The instance achieves a top 10% rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU tests, including physics and encryption workloads. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 7743.53 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and maintaining strong performance of 273.18 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core performance is strong to average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1666.0 and average single-threaded compression speeds.

The server demonstrates high throughput in database and web serving operations, processing 120.67 million Redis SET operations per second and delivering 48.38 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. For machine learning workloads, it offers strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium models, though text generation for large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, performing at a weak 2.74 tokens per second. This profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for highly parallelized compute workloads, ray tracing, and database operations that leverage its dense multi-core architecture without requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 112 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, 56 physical cores, and 448 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance is designed for high-throughput parallel computing. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, outstanding memory bandwidth of up to 7743.53 GB/sec for cached reads, and exceptional capabilities in cryptography and physics simulations. It processes 120.67 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers strong static web serving throughput. While single-core performance and large-model text generation are more moderate, its dense core count and high memory bandwidth make it highly efficient for software compilation, ray tracing, and parallel database workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is an x86_64 server with 112 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 448 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is an x86_64 server with 112 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 448 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput compute.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core x86_64 compute platform optimized for parallel processing, high memory bandwidth, and demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 112 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors with 448 GB of RAM
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth up to 7743.53 GB/sec
Excellent throughput for database operations, handling 120.67 million Redis SET operations per second
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and highly parallelized compute workloads
